summary: PHP -> JSON can't tell if array() is an empty {} or empty []
$in with empty array throws an error, should it?
description:
As I've understood it using an empty array shouldn't throw an error. Using the below it does.

INPUT
Array pushed to MongoCollection::findOne:

array (
  'oldId' => 
  array (
    '$in' => 
    array (
    ),
  ),
)


OUTPUT
array (
  '$err' => '$in requires array',
)

summary: paired connection doesn't handle ports properly
PHP: MongoCursor does not return MongoID objects but Strings
MongoGridFS::storeFile and MongoGridFS::storeBytes return different things.
description:
These two functions return different things ("Returns if the bytes were saved." vs. "Returns the database id for the file.").
Would be more consistent if they both returned the same thing, since they essentially do the same thing.

http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongogridfs.storebytes.php
http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongogridfs.storefile.php

querying specific fields using findOne(array("_id"=>$idUser),array("myfield"=>1)) gives a warning:

Warning: MongoCollection::findOne() expects at most 1 parameter, 2 given

http://us2.php.net/manual/en/mongocollection.count.php

This page lists that an integer is returned. Then, in the example further down a flot is mentioned as output.

summary: Error checking always increases memory usage
description: On every Mongo::lastError execution the memory footprint increases. I tested to reduce memory with Mongo::resetError. But this needs even more memory. I have a complex PHP script which needs without error checking about 2MB and with error checking on every insert, update and save (it's about 500000 operations) it needs about 105MB. Nothing else changed. It seems (after some tests) that every error checking operation (also indirectly with MongoDB::execute) increases memory usage of the PHP process (memory_get_usage reports increased memory usage after every operation). Is there any workaround i've overseen.

summary: PHP driver produces "getMore: cursorid not found" in mongod output
description:
The following test script produces the error "Sat Aug 29 20:36:34 getMore: cursorid not found test.testcollection 0."  The equivalent JS script does not.

PHP:

$mongo = new Mongo();                                                                               

$collection = $mongo->selectDB("test")->selectCollection("testcollection");                         

$collection->drop();                                                                                
                                                                                                    
$collection->save(array("name" => "Foo"));                                                          

$cursor = $collection->find(array("name" => "Foo"));                                                

$cursor->hasNext();                                                                                 
$cursor->getNext();                                                                                 
$cursor->hasNext();                                                                                 

JS:

db.testcollection.drop();                                                                           

db.testcollection.save({ name: "Foo" });                                                            

var c = db.testcollection.find({ name: "Foo" });                                                    

c.hasNext();                                                                                        
c.next();                                                                                           
c.hasNext();

summary: PHP Driver will not compile on FreeBSD without sys/types.h include
description:
It seems that FreeBSD requires the sys/types.h header in conjunction with netinet/tcp.h in mongo.c to compile successfully.

Error without sys/types.h included:

[matt@sihnon ~/build/mongodb-mongo-php-driver-e6458ed46bf8d300b487583e77885adf2f7f6e80]$ make

summary: Exception on MongoCursor::find with $in containing huge amount of elements
driver doesn't check if it's connected before running ismaster command
description: See http://groups.google.com/group/mongodb-user/browse_thread/thread/3af1584167485f4c

summary: $ conflicts with php's $, a pretty significat distraction for php developers
description:
Hi

I'm sure this has been raised several times but I figured what the heck.

The $, can we have that renamed to different symbol like @, that $ dollar sign is php's main symbol for variables.
It really bothers me because I'd like to have mongo just have a beautiful language that works with php. I like everything about mongo just except that $ which messes it all up in the code-uniformity department with php.

Any thoughts?
Please, add MongoTimestamp.
there is an index on { n : 1 }, but it should be on { files_id : 1 , n : 1 }

On client side PHP code die with:
MongoCursorException: couldn't send query.

On server side in console:
Mon Sep 21 22:02:21 connection accepted from 192.168.1.33:14792 #6
Mon Sep 21 22:02:21 bad recv() len: -1543503872
Mon Sep 21 22:02:21 end connection 192.168.1.33:14792

PHP code:
$collection = $db->selectCollection('feeds');
$collection->insert($feed);  // goes OK
		
$cursor = $collection->find();  // goes OK
foreach ($cursor as $id => $value) {  // MongoCursor->rewind(  ) throws an exception
   echo "$id: ";
   var_dump( $value );
}

Code above runs fine on Ubuntu, but failed on Mac OS X with Power PC.
Using mongodb 1.1.0 as server (Ubuntu netbook remix 9.04), using as client:
1) actual version from http://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver (21 september 2009)
2) 0.9.5 from http://pecl.php.net/package/mongo

Client environment:

PHP 5.3.0 (cli) (built: Jul 31 2009 16:39:26)
Apache/2.2.12 (Unix) (Jul 31 2009 14:32:52)

uname -a
Darwin melfimov.local 9.8.0 Darwin Kernel Version 9.8.0: Wed Jul 15 16:57:01 PDT 2009; root:xnu-1228.15.4~1/RELEASE_PPC Power Macintosh

phpize
PHP Api Version:         20090626
Zend Module Api No:      20090626
Zend Extension Api No:   220090626

summary: use db url format for connecting
description:
Instead of Mongo taking a dizzying array of parameters, have everything in the server string:

mongo://[uname:pass@]server1[:port][,server2[:port]]

while maintaining backwards compat
Don't break backwards compatibility.

summary: associative array keys should be strings
"Fatal error: Nesting level too deep - recursive dependency?"
description:
Hi, Kristina!
I got the error  "Fatal error: Nesting level too deep - recursive dependency?" inside of the Mongo() method. It happens not all of the time, just sometimes. The same with "Fatal error: Cannot access protected property Mongo::$connections" (this text isn't exact).
Faults of segmentation is annoying.
I can provide you access to development server if necessary.

summary: Error creating mongo object in static method
Seg fault when calling method on data returned from MongoCursor
description:
Calling a method on data returned by MongoCursor leads to segmentation fault. 

Reproduce code:
-----------------------
$collection = $mongo->selectCollection('test', 'test'); 
$result = $collection->find()->limit(5); // collection must have at least 6 records

foreach ($result as $obj) {
    echo $obj->someMethod();
}


Expected result:
---------------------
PHP Fatal error:  Call to a member function someMethod() on a non-object in test.php on line 7

summary: Allow spaces after commas in urls

description: VC6 build isn't working with Apache (XAMPP?)

summary: Passing entire javascript functions for group
force empty query to be an obj

description:
Currently, the php driver tries to connect to the specified host and timeouts in 20 seconds, which is hardcoded.

It would be good if this limit was configurable by passing it as an option to the constructor (as milliseconds or something, in case someone wants it to timeout in less than a second).
If nothing is passed, it could use the `default_socket_timeout` configuration option.

Using pairs/sets of servers and one of them failing causes a huge slowdown now, because of that 20 second timeout.

* make sure autoreconnect logins happen
* support /db syntax

summary: Fatal error on memory limit (trying to alloc 4Gb) if user without password specified in connection string
description:
I'm trying to connect to mongodb:

> php -r 'var_dump(new Mongo("mongodb://admin@anyhost-and-nonexistent"));'

Fatal error: Allowed memory size of 536870912 bytes exhausted (tried to allocate 4275929599 bytes) in Command line code on line 1

Call Stack:
    0.0014      92168   1. {main}() Command line code:0
    0.0015      93464   2. Mongo->__construct(string(39)) Command line code:1

Segmentation fault (core dumped) // <--- bug #PHP-87

summary: It would be great if it was possible to use array methods like in_array and array_search over arrays of MongoId objects
The MongoCollection docs need to mention $
description:
Collection names can use any character in the ASCII set per the PHP manual that's not so, it's the ASCII set minus $. I tested this with the shell but also the PECL mongodb source code for listCollections reflects this as it checks for the character in a system.namespaces entry to see that it's not an index: // check that the ns is valid and not an index (contains $).

How can the ns be invalid, I am curious? Someone manually writes into system.namespaces?

The PHP driver regularly crashes on my development system.  The code is complex and I am not sure how to reproduce exactly.  I cannot reproduce the error when I only have 1 PHP process or if I am running it under a debugger so I assume it is some sort of concurrency issue.

I am using persistent connections.

Below are 2 backtraces that I have generated from core dumps, let me know if there is any more information I can provide.

summary: Double free in php_mongo_write_update, php_mongo_write_delete etc
Using $set with nested array does not work
description:
<?php
$m = new Mongo();
$db = $m->selectDB('checkout');
$c = $db->selectCollection('merchant');

$c->insert(array('_id'=>'testid', 'config'=>array()));
print_r($c->findOne(array('_id'=>'testid')));

$c->update(array('_id'=>'testid'), array('$set'=>array('config'=>array('test_test'=>array('array_test'=>null))), array('upsert'=>true)));

print_r($c->findOne(array('_id'=>'testid')));
?>

This should update the config array but it does not.  I think the code should be self-explanatory.

Here is the output on my machine

Array
(
    [_id] => testid
    [config] => Array
        (
        )

)

Array
(
    [_id] => testid
    [config] => Array
        (
        )

)

$_REQUEST=iterator_to_array(<MongoCursor>,FALSE); // <<-- works fine

But the PHP global receiving the array is not always $_REQUEST. PHP doesn't allow variable variables involving globals either, so eval is the only option here.

eval('$_'.$phpglobal.'=iterator_to_array(<MongoCursor>,FALSE);'); <<-- generates the MongoId::__set_state() error
